source_evidence: Eq. (9): accept bar pq if d_hat_{p,q} . d_hat_{p,i} < ColTol for all i, else reject. 'The user-defined ColTol <= 1 controls the admissible collinearity... the minimum angle between generated bars is alpha >= acos(ColTol).' Footnote: dot product of directional cosines equals cosine of the angle between them.
purpose: Rejects newly generated candidate bars that are (nearly) collinear/overlapping with members already in the ground structure, preventing redundant duplicate members.
assumptions: New bars added at level n are deleted if collinear with bars from previous levels
New bars at level n are assumed not collinear between themselves (checked only against previously generated bars)
New bars are longer than lower-level bars, so the newer bar is eliminated
Directional cosine vectors have unit norm so their dot product is the cosine of the angle between them
formula_or_algorithm: For candidate bar pq with direction d_hat_{p,q}, compute dot product with every relevant existing bar direction d_hat_{p,i}. Accept pq if d_hat_{p,q} . d_hat_{p,i} < ColTol for all i; otherwise reject. The minimum admissible angle is alpha = acos(ColTol), with ColTol <= 1 (e.g. 0.999999).
validation_opportunities: unit test with deliberately collinear bars

reuse_risk.explanation: Clear rule, but the 'check against previously generated only' assumption can be violated for non-strictly-convex base meshes or curling/spiraling expansions, and the tolerance choice affects results.

missing_information: Exact set of 'previously generated' bars compared against (indexing/order)
